series_title_en: CBT Core for Children - Stage 2: Thoughts

items.therapeutic_goal: Help the child notice a thought as words in the mind and choose a kinder thought.
items.when_to_use: Use when a child needs help naming a thought and noticing whether it feels helpful.
items.ai_matching_summary: The child chooses a possible thought, notices how it feels, checks whether it is helpful, and selects a kinder thought.
items.safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, suicide, or trauma content. Wording stays calm, hopeful, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Teach the child to separate what happened from what the mind is guessing.
items.when_to_use: Use when a child mixes facts with guesses or worries about what others think.
items.ai_matching_summary: The worksheet helps distinguish real events from guesses, then choose a more balanced idea.
items.safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, suicide, or trauma content. Wording stays calm, hopeful, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Help the child notice worry thoughts kindly and choose simple coping supports.
items.when_to_use: Use when a child has worry thoughts about mistakes, peers, or trying something hard.
items.ai_matching_summary: The child identifies what worry says, how worry feels, and what helps when worry appears.
items.safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, suicide, or trauma content. Wording stays calm, hopeful, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Practice changing an unhelpful thought into a kinder and more useful thought.
items.when_to_use: Use when a child is stuck in an unhelpful thought such as "I cannot do this" or "I will mess up".
items.ai_matching_summary: The child matches unhelpful thoughts with kinder replacements and fills in a personal thought switch.
items.safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, suicide, or trauma content. Wording stays calm, hopeful, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Encourage the child to look for clues before believing every thought.
items.when_to_use: Use when a child needs to check assumptions and identify what they know for sure.
items.ai_matching_summary: The child names a thought, identifies facts and guesses, and chooses what else could be true.
items.safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, suicide, or trauma content. Wording stays calm, hopeful, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Build a helpful thought the child can use to move forward.
items.when_to_use: Use when a child needs a simple replacement thought before taking a next step.
items.ai_matching_summary: The child notices a feeling, chooses a thought that helps, selects what supports the next action, and writes a personal helpful thought.
items.safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, suicide, or trauma content. Wording stays calm, hopeful, and age-appropriate.
